Title: Kiwifruit MYBS1-like and GBF3 transcription factors influence L-ascorbic acid biosynthesis by activating transcription of GDP-L-galactose phosphorylase 3

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: L-galactose pathway of ascorbic acid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key biosynthetic control step for AsA accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts GDP-L-galactose to L-galactose-1-phosphate in the L-galactose pathway of ascorbic acid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms a direct precursor in the AsA biosynthetic pathway and strongly controls flux to the target product
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Essential human micronutrient; contributes to plant abiotic stress resilience
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes AsA accumulation; overexpression strongly increases AsA while knockout/silencing reduces AsA
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AceGGP3 was the most highly expressed GGP homolog in high-AsA Actinidia eriantha fruit and its expression positively correlated with fruit AsA content. 2. Transient and stable overexpression of AceGGP3 increased AsA accumulation markedly in A. eriantha calli and fruit. 3. CRISPR/Cas9 mutagenesis or antisense silencing of AceGGP3 reduced AsA content, demonstrating causal control of AsA biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq correlation; qRT-PCR; transient overexpression; antisense silencing; stable overexpression; CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; metabolite profiling by HPLC
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed during early fruit development; most highly expressed GGP in A. eriantha fruit
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High in early developing fruit; transgene-expressing calli and fruit show elevated transcript abundance
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: AceMYBS1; AceGBF3 (regulatory factors acting on promoter/expression)
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: ABA indirectly represses AceGGP3 expression through repression of AceMYBS1
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter (constitutive) for overexpression in transgenic and transient assays
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate target to elevate vitamin C in kiwifruit and other crops; likely useful in metabolic engineering because it is a major control point
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: A mutant line (ggp3#11) retained near-WT AsA despite a frameshift, likely because downstream translation initiation generated a functional N-terminally truncated protein.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: MYBS1-like MYB protein with two SANT/MYB regions; predicted N-terminal histone-interacting SANT domain and central MYB DNA-binding domain
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Essential human micronutrient; contributes to plant abiotic stress resilience
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive regulator that increases AsA accumulation by activating AceGGP3 transcription
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Direct transcriptional activator of the key control gene AceGGP3 and major positive regulator of AsA accumulation; required for ABA responsiveness of this module
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Direct transcriptional activation of AceGGP3 promoter; protein-protein interaction with AceGBF3 to enhance activation
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Ascorbic acid biosynthesis through the L-galactose pathway
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms an activating complex/module with AceGBF3; physical interaction supported by Y2H, BiFC, BiLC and pull-down
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed during early fruit development; positively correlated with AceGGP3 expression and AsA content
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High in early developing fruit; manipulated in fruit and calli
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq/qRT-PCR correlation; Y1H; Dual-LUC; EMSA; transient overexpression; antisense silencing; stable overexpression; CRISPR/Cas9 editing; metabolite profiling; BiFC/BiLC/pull-down for partner interaction
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AceMYBS1 expression strongly correlates with AceGGP3 expression and AsA accumulation in A. eriantha fruit. 2. AceMYBS1 directly binds two motifs in the AceGGP3 promoter and activates its transcription. 3. Overexpression of AceMYBS1 increases AceGGP3 expression and AsA content, while silencing or CRISPR editing decreases AsA. 4. AceMYBS1 acts upstream of AceGGP3 because AceMYBS1 overexpression cannot raise AsA when AceGGP3 is suppressed. 5. ABA represses AceMYBS1, thereby lowering AceGGP3 expression and AsA accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: ABA treatment represses AceMYBS1 and reduces AsA accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter (constitutive) for overexpression in stable and transient assays
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising regulatory target for boosting vitamin C in kiwifruit; could be combined with AceGBF3 or GGP3 for stronger enhancement; caution that ABA signaling may counteract effect
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: A closely related MYBS1 on chr26 lies within a high-AsA QTL interval but was not the main experimentally validated gene in this paper.

Regulation_Genes.Regulator_Type: bZIP transcription factor; G-box binding factor
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Essential human micronutrient; contributes to plant abiotic stress resilience
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive regulator that increases AsA accumulation additively with AceMYBS1 by promoting AceGGP3 expression
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Conditionally important co-activator that boosts AceMYBS1-dependent activation of AceGGP3 and AsA synthesis
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Ascorbic acid biosynthesis through regulation of AceGGP3 expression
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Promotes AceGGP3 expression; additive enhancement with AceMYBS1
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Interacts with AceMYBS1; N-terminal region of AceGBF3 mediates interaction
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: ABA did not significantly repress AceGBF3 expression, but ABA reduced AceMYBS1-AceGBF3 functional output
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expression correlated with AceGGP3 during developmental stages
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Manipulated in fruit, calli, and tobacco leaves
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y2H screening and mapping; subcellular localization; BiFC; BiLC; pull-down; Dual-LUC; transient overexpression/silencing; stable overexpression; metabolite profiling
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AceGBF3 was identified as an AceMYBS1-interacting bZIP factor whose expression correlates with AceGGP3. 2. AceGBF3 physically interacts with AceMYBS1 in vivo and in vitro. 3. Overexpression of AceGBF3 increases AceGGP3 transcript abundance and AsA content in fruit, calli and tobacco leaves. 4. Co-expression of AceGBF3 with AceMYBS1 additively enhances AceGGP3 activation and AsA accumulation. 5. AceGBF3 requires AceMYBS1 for full regulatory effect because AceGBF3 overexpression cannot rescue AsA when AceMYBS1 is silenced.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: ABA reduces the AceMYBS1-AceGBF3 activation output, but AceGBF3 transcript itself is not significantly repressed by ABA
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter (constitutive) for overexpression in stable and transient assays
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful co-regulator for stacking with AceMYBS1 to further increase vitamin C and possibly improve abiotic stress resilience
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: AceGBF3 alone had limited promoter activation in one Dual-LUC context, but consistently enhanced AsA accumulation and showed additive action with AceMYBS1 across assays.
